sla_text_cache:
Service Level Agreement (SLA) 
Document Information 
 Document Name: Demo SLA 
 Version: 1.0 
 Effective Date: [DD/MM/YYYY] 
 Owner: Sales / Pre-Sales Team 
1. Purpose 
This Service Level Agreement (SLA) defines the service standards for scheduling, preparing, 
delivering, and following up on product demonstrations for prospective customers. 
2. Scope 
This SLA applies to: 
 Product demonstrations 
 Proof-of-Concept (PoC) discussions 
 Technical walkthroughs 
 Customer Q&A sessions 
3. Service Levels 
Activity SLA Target 
Acknowledge demo request Within 4 business hours 
Schedule demo Within 2 business days 
Share meeting invitation At least 24 hours before demo 
Conduct demo As per agreed schedule 
Share presentation/material Within 1 business day after demo 
Respond to post-demo queries Within 1 business day 
Submit follow-up summary Within 2 business days 
4. Customer Responsibilities 
The customer should: 
 Provide business requirements before the demo. 
 Share the list of attendees. 
 Confirm the preferred date and time. 

 Ensure internet connectivity for virtual demos. 
 Notify any cancellations at least 24 hours in advance. 
5. Service Provider Responsibilities 
The service provider will: 
 Assign a qualified presenter. 
 Customize the demo where feasible. 
 Demonstrate agreed product features. 
 Address customer questions. 
 Provide relevant documentation after the session. 
6. Exclusions 
The following are outside the scope of this SLA: 
 Product customization during the demo 
 Production issue resolution 
 Development commitments 
 Formal training sessions 
 Guaranteed feature implementation 
7. Escalation Matrix 
Level Contact Response Time 
Level 1 Pre-Sales Executive 4 business hours 
Level 2 Pre-Sales Manager 1 business day 
Level 3 Sales Director 2 business days 
8. Availability 
 Business Hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM 
 Public holidays excluded unless otherwise agreed. 
9. Performance Metrics 
 Demo scheduling compliance: ≥95% 
 On-time demo delivery: ≥98% 
 Customer satisfaction score: ≥4.5/5 
 Follow-up completion: ≥95% 

10. Review 
This SLA will be reviewed annually or whenever significant changes are made to the demo 
process. 
11. Approval 
Role Name  Signature Date 
Sales Manager    Pre-Sales Manager    Customer Representative

sla_text_cache:
Damaged Product Eligibility Policy 
Document Information 
 Document Name: Damaged Product Eligibility Policy 
 Version: 1.0 
 Effective Date: [DD/MM/YYYY] 
 Owner: Quality Assurance / Customer Support 
1. Purpose 
This policy defines the eligibility criteria for accepting claims related to damaged products and 
outlines the conditions under which a product qualifies for replacement, repair, refund, or credit. 
2. Scope 
This policy applies to: 
 Products damaged during shipping 
 Manufacturing defects identified upon delivery 
 Transit damage 
 Incorrectly packaged products 
 Products received in unusable condition 
3. Eligibility Criteria 
A damaged product is eligible for review if all of the following conditions are met: 
 The damage is reported within 7 calendar days of delivery. 
 Proof of purchase is provided. 
 Photographs or videos clearly showing the damage are submitted. 
 The original packaging is retained where applicable. 
 The product has not been intentionally damaged or improperly used. 
 The product serial number or identification label is intact. 
4. Eligible Damage Types 
The following damage types may qualify: 
 Physical breakage 
 Cracks or dents 

 Missing components 
 Water damage during shipment 
 Manufacturing defects 
 Packaging damage resulting in product damage 
5. Non-Eligible Cases 
Claims may be rejected if: 
 The damage is caused by customer misuse or negligence. 
 The claim is submitted after the reporting period. 
 Normal wear and tear is reported as damage. 
 The product has been modified or repaired by an unauthorized party. 
 No supporting evidence is provided. 
6. Required Documentation 
Customers should submit: 
 Order number or invoice 
 Product serial number (if applicable) 
 Clear photographs/videos of the damaged product 
 Images of the shipping package 
 Description of the issue 
7. Claim Review Process 
1. Customer submits a damage claim. 
2. Customer Support verifies the documentation. 
3. Quality Assurance reviews the claim. 
4. Eligibility is determined. 
5. Customer is notified of the decision. 
6. If approved, replacement, repair, refund, or store credit is processed. 
8. Resolution Options 
Eligible claims may result in: 
 Product replacement 
 Product repair 
 Full refund 
 Partial refund 

 Store credit 
9. SLA 
Activity Target 
Claim acknowledgment Within 1 business day 
Initial review Within 2 business days 
Final decision Within 5 business days 
Replacement shipment Within 3 business days after approval 
10. Exceptions 
Exceptions may be granted for: 
 Carrier-confirmed shipping damage 
 Manufacturer recalls 
 Large commercial orders with contractual terms 
 Other cases approved by management 
11. Approval 
Role Name  Signature Date 
Quality Manager    Customer Support Manager    Operations Manager
